cases.synopsis: Marcus T. is a 15-year-old referred by his school because of long-standing academic and daily-living concerns. Since early elementary school, he has struggled to keep up with age-level learning. He reads at approximately a second- to third-grade level and needs new information broken into simple steps and repeated many times. Even with support and extra time, he often forgets instructions from one day to the next.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Marcus has struggled since early elementary school to keep up with age-level learning. He reads at approximately a second- to third-grade level and needs new information broken into simple steps and repeated many times.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: School and intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Even with support and extra time, Marcus often forgets instructions from one day to the next. These difficulties have been present since childhood and occur across school, home, and community settings.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Socially, Marcus is friendly but struggles to follow conversations, understand jokes or sarcasm, and participate independently.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: At home, Marcus requires regular assistance with age-expected tasks. He can dress himself and complete basic hygiene routines, but he often forgets steps unless reminded.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Marcus has difficulty understanding money, planning ahead, and responding to unexpected situations.

hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.

cases.synopsis: Lena S. is a 9-year-old who has been described as clumsy since early childhood. She frequently bumps into objects, drops items, and struggles with tasks requiring coordination. These difficulties occur daily at school and at home.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Lena has been described as clumsy since early childhood. She frequently bumps into objects, drops items, and struggles with tasks requiring coordination.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: School and intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Her handwriting is slow and difficult to read, and her hand becomes tired during written assignments. She avoids drawing and writing when possible.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: During physical activities, Lena has trouble catching balls, maintaining balance, and coordinating movements.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: At home, dressing takes longer than expected, especially when tying shoes or fastening buttons.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Lena understands instructions, learns new concepts, and performs well academically when tasks do not depend heavily on writing or physical coordination. Her motor difficulties have been present for several years and are beginning to affect her confidence and willingness to participate.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.

cases.presentingConcern: Withdrawal, depressive symptoms, and psychotic symptoms
cases.referralSource: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
cases.primaryGoal: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
cases.synopsis: Anthony R. is a 28-year-old who has shown increasing withdrawal and unusual behavior over the past six months. During several multi-week periods, he experiences depressed mood, low energy, loss of interest, difficulty getting out of bed, impaired concentration, and hopelessness.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: During several multi-week periods, Anthony experiences depressed mood, low energy, loss of interest, difficulty getting out of bed, impaired concentration, and hopelessness.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Anthony reports hearing a voice that comments negatively on his actions. He believes others may be watching or following him and has begun avoiding leaving home.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: These psychotic symptoms occur multiple times per week and sometimes daily.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: There is no evidence that substance use or a medical condition explains the symptoms.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Family members report that the hallucinations and suspiciousness continue even when Anthony's mood improves. There have been periods lasting approximately two weeks when he was less depressed but continued to hear voices and act suspiciously.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: Anthony has stopped working and rarely interacts with friends or family.

cases.synopsis: Rachel M. is a 34-year-old who has felt worried and tense almost every day for the past two years. She worries about numerous areas of her life, including work, finances, health, and family. She finds the worry difficult to control, and one concern quickly shifts to another.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Rachel spends several hours each day anticipating potential problems and preparing for worst-case scenarios. Even when nothing is wrong, she feels uneasy and expects something bad may happen.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: The worry interferes with Rachel's concentration at work and her ability to relax.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Her symptoms occur across many situations and remain relatively consistent rather than appearing in brief episodes.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: She experiences muscle tension, fatigue, restlessness, irritability, mental exhaustion, and difficulty sleeping.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.

cases.synopsis: Evan L. is a 25-year-old with a long-standing pattern of difficulty connecting with others. Since his late teens, others have described him as unusual or difficult to understand. He believes certain events or coincidences carry special personal meaning and that he can sense things others cannot.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Evan believes certain events or coincidences carry special personal meaning and that he can sense things others cannot.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: His speech may be vague, abstract, or difficult to follow, and he dresses and behaves in unconventional ways.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: He feels uncomfortable in social situations and often assumes others are judging him or talking about him without sufficient evidence.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: These patterns have remained consistent for several years.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Evan has few close relationships, although he does express some desire for connection. His beliefs are unusual, but he can sometimes question them when challenged.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: He does not report persistent hallucinations or fully losing contact with reality.

cases.synopsis: Kayla J. is a 5-year-old living with a foster family after experiencing multiple caregiver changes during early childhood. During her first three years, she moved between several homes and did not have a consistent caregiver.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Kayla frequently approaches unfamiliar adults with excessive familiarity and little caution. She walks up to strangers, begins conversations easily, and may seek physical closeness without hesitation.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: These behaviors occur in stores, parks, school, and other settings.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: When she is with her caregivers, Kayla may wander away to interact with unfamiliar adults without checking back or demonstrating awareness of safety concerns.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: During her first three years, Kayla moved between several homes and did not have a consistent caregiver.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: She treats unfamiliar adults much like familiar caregivers. These behaviors have continued for over a year despite placement in a more stable and supportive home.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
